improvements.accuracy: Minor date precision: body lists v2.1.98 as '(4/8 前後)' but the changelog dates it April 9, 2026. The hedge particle '前後' makes this defensible, but '(4/9)' would be exact. Frontmatter summary 'Claude Code 在四月上旬三天內連出三版（v2.1.94 → v2.1.101）' compresses 4/7→4/10 (4 calendar days) into '三天'; consider '四天' or simply '四月上旬連出三版'. The ClawdNote on /dev/tcp says '最早的書面記錄可以追溯到 2003 年前後' — bash /dev/tcp redirection has actually shipped since bash 2.04 (March 2000); 2003-era penetration-testing write-ups are when the technique entered red-team folklore, so the framing is defensible but slightly understates the feature's age. Optional polish: '2000 年前後就是 bash 內建功能、CTF 圈 2003 年起常見引用'.
improvements.fidelity: All material claims map cleanly to changelog text: '~50 MB/hr MCP HTTP/SSE leak', 'virtual scroller dozens-of-copies leak (fixed v2.1.101)', '60% faster Write tool diff on tabs/&/$ (v2.1.92)', four bypasses (backslash-escape, compound bash, /dev/tcp & /dev/udp, POSIX `which` LSP injection), v2.1.98 vs v2.1.101 grouping, named-subagent typeahead in v2.1.89, Monitor tool in v2.1.98, /team-onboarding in v2.1.101, default effort raised to high. The 'Live agent count indicator' framing in the closing paragraph is a paraphrase of the changelog's '● N running indicator in /agents' and the '/agents tabbed Running tab' improvement — fair compression. The article's mechanistic explanation of the backslash bypass ('permission 系統在做字串匹配時，沒有考慮反斜線轉義的正規化') is plausible inference rather than a quoted root cause, but does not contradict the changelog.
improvements.consistency: Timeline is now internally consistent: bypasses described as existing 'v2.1.98 之前', three patched in v2.1.98, LSP injection patched in v2.1.101 — matches the per-version changelog grouping. Memory-leak narrative (MCP layer + virtual scroller, both fixed by v2.1.101) is coherent. Earlier draft's v2.1.97 contradiction has been resolved.
